name: Los Angeles to Houston LTL
hero-headline: Los Angeles, CA to Houston, TX LTL Freight
subheadline: 1,618 miles. 5-7 business day transit. 11 carriers active on this lane.
body-content:
Lane Overview: Los Angeles to Houston

The Los Angeles to Houston LTL lane covers approximately 1,618 miles between the West Coast and South Central regions. Standard transit runs 5-7 business days depending on carrier routing and terminal schedules. Common equipment on this corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at LA Gateway, Inland Empire, Bay Area Hub support consolidation and transit optimization. Peak volumes Aug-Oct (produce season). Rate pressure Dec-Jan.

How WARP Operates the Los Angeles to Houston Lane

WARP covers the Los Angeles to Houston corridor with 11 vetted carriers providing LTL capacity.

  • Service capabilities on this lane include: Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery; Estimated 5-7 business day transit window; Pallet-level tracking with delivery appointment scheduling.
  • Shipments receive pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery with appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation.
  • Nearby cross-dock operations at LA Gateway and Inland Empire enable consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur.
  • Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change.
  • WARP's operations team manages carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution without requiring shipper follow-up.

Operating Details

Key operational considerations for this lane:

  • Estimated lane distance: 1,618 miles (Los Angeles to Houston)
  • Transit window: 5-7 business days for standard LTL service
  • Available equipment: Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled)
  • Active carriers on this lane: 11
  • Nearest cross-dock facilities: LA Gateway, Inland Empire, Bay Area Hub, Houston Terminal, Dallas Mega Hub
  • Real-time scan events at pickup, in-transit, and delivery milestones
  • Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes with automated escalation
  • Delivery appointment scheduling and confirmation managed by WARP operations

Pricing & Rate Factors

Estimated LTL rates on the Los Angeles to Houston lane range from $4,831 to $6,441, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand.

  • Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and whether accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup apply.
  • At 1,618 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $3.
  • 48/mile).
  • Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices.
  • These are modeled estimates.

Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Validate This Lane

Validate this lane with a controlled pilot: Los Angeles to Houston LTL (1,618-mile corridor; 5–7 day transit window; 11 vetted carriers active; cross-dock access at LA Gateway and Inland Empire). Track quote response time, transit predictability, and exception trends before scaling volume. Start with a single shipment to benchmark WARP against your current process.

faq-schema:
How much does LTL freight from Los Angeles, CA to Houston, TX cost?

Estimated LTL rates range from $4,831 to $6,441 based on freight class, weight, and pallet count. Actual rates depend on shipment specifics and current market conditions. Get an instant quote for real-time pricing.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Los Angeles to Houston?

Standard LTL transit on this 1,618-mile lane is 5-7 business days. Actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Los Angeles to Houston lane?

WARP maintains a vetted network of approximately 11 active carriers on this corridor. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Los Angeles to Houston in real time?

Yes. Every shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Los Angeles to Houston corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.
